title: Long-Term Legal Stay for Digital Nomads and Freelancers - Georgia
summary: US citizens and many other nationals can stay in Georgia visa-free for up to one year and may structure freelance activity through local registration routes.
steps: Enter Georgia with an eligible passport under visa-free rules.
Register local address where required by current local process.
For freelance work, register as an Individual Entrepreneur (IE) with the Revenue Service.
Apply for Small Business Status where eligible for reduced tax treatment.
Open a Georgian bank account for local operations.
For stays beyond visa-free period, apply for residence permit using the applicable legal category.
documents: For IE registration: passport and tax registration details
For bank account: identity and address proof requested by bank
For residence permit: financial means and legal basis documentation
office: Revenue Service of Georgia for business registration and Public Service Hall / migration authorities for residence matters.
fee: Registration and service fees vary by channel and urgency
notes: Georgia is outside the EU framework and rules can differ materially from EU migration pathways.
